contact: Apply Now Eligibility VJNT category students studying in Class 11 and above Region India Award A monthly maintenance allowance up to ₹425 and other benefits Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships post-matric-scholarship-to-vjnt-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Post-Matric Scholarship to VJNT Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra to financially help the students studying in Class 11 and higher. The scheme enables the Vimukta Jati and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category students to pursue higher education by providing them with a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹425 and other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Vimukta Jati and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category be studying in Class 11 and higher in courses by the government have secured 75% attendance for the current year have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹1,50,000 Benefits The selected scholars will receive the maintenance allowance as follows:- Course Hostlers (₹ per month) Day Scholars (₹ per month) Group A 425 190 Group B 290 190 Group C 290 190 Group D 230 120 Group E 150 90 Note:- The scholarship amount will be given to the students from the admission date to till exam completion date. The students will receive 100% of the tuition fee, exam fee, and maintenance allowance to study in government/government-aided/non-aided institutions in professional and non-professional courses. If the applicant takes an admission on or before the 20th of any month, the maintenance allowance will be issued for that current month. Otherwise, maintenance allowance will be entitled from next month. 100% benefit (tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance) is applicable for D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses. For students studying in government-aided /non-aided D.Ed. and B.Ed courses, the fee structure is applicable as per government rates for the same course. Maintenance allowance and exam fees are disbursed to the applicant upon promotion to the next higher class. Documents Caste certificate issued by a competent authority Income certificate/declaration issued by a competent authority Caste validity certificate Marksheet of HSC or SSC or last examination Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Father/Guardian's death certificate (if applicable) Ration card Leaving certificate Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Note:- Caste validity certificate is mandatory for professional degree courses and professional postgraduate courses. However, it is not mandatory for non-professional courses. A maximum of 2 male children from a family are allowed to avail of this scholarship. For female children, this rule does not apply. How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines Terms and Conditions The applicants are required to pass the previous year's examination for the renewal of the scholarship. In the case of VJNT, if an applicant fails for a year, no reimbursement is provided for that specific year. If the applicants get admission to a government hostel, they will be eligible for one-third amount of hostlers. Applicants should come through the CAP round for professional courses. Scholarships/freeship will continue until the applicant completes one course. If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level. No scholarship will be paid to the applicants under this scheme who are already receiving a scholarship/stipend from another source. The applicants who change from professional to non-professional courses will not receive any scholarship. If an applicant changes their course in between academic years, they will not be eligible for further assistance. If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim. Scholarships or freeships will continue to be provided to students until they complete one course, such as 11th or 12th grade Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., or Ph.D. If a student completes both B.A. and B.Ed. degrees and later decide to pursue an M.A., they will no longer be eligible for scholarship/freeship benefits. However, if a student opts for an M.B.A. following their B.Ed., they may be eligible for scholarship or freeship support, as it is considered a professional postgraduate course. For Group A, if an applicant pursuing a Group A course fails in the examination for the first time, the award may be renewed. However, for second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class. For Group B, C, D, and E, the applicant must secure a promotion to a higher class. In case of second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class. Contact Details VJNT, OBC and SBC Welfare Department, Maharashtra 3 Church Road, Pune 1 Email ID: jdvjnt@maharashtra.gov.in | Phone Number: 18001208040 Source : https://mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

title: Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to VJNT Students, Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: Up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees
eligibility: VJNT category students studying at post-matric level
description: Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to VJNT Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, for Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category students pursuing post-matric education (from Class 11 and above). The scholarship provides financial aid for pursuing higher education. The selected scholars will receive up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees.
eligibility_full: VJNT category students studying at post-matric level Region India Award Up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships tuition-fees-and-examination-fees-to-vjnt-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to VJNT Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, for Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category students pursuing post-matric education (from Class 11 and above). The scholarship provides financial aid for pursuing higher education. The selected scholars will receive up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category be pursuing a government-approved course at the post-matric level (Class 11 and above) in a government/government-aided/private institution have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) process (for professional courses) have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Note:- The applicants must have been admitted to government, government-aided, private non-aided, or private permanently non-aided institutions for professional courses. If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. Applicants who are admitted to deemed universities for professional courses are not applicable.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive benefits in the following manner:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) 100 % benefit will be given to the students enrolled in D.Ed and B.Ed courses. The fee structure for students in aided or unaided D.Ed and B.Ed courses follows government rates for the same course.
documents: Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026
selection_criteria: The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi)
contact: Apply Now Eligibility VJNT category students studying at post-matric level Region India Award Up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships tuition-fees-and-examination-fees-to-vjnt-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to VJNT Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, for Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category students pursuing post-matric education (from Class 11 and above). The scholarship provides financial aid for pursuing higher education. The selected scholars will receive up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category be pursuing a government-approved course at the post-matric level (Class 11 and above) in a government/government-aided/private institution have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) process (for professional courses) have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Note:- The applicants must have been admitted to government, government-aided, private non-aided, or private permanently non-aided institutions for professional courses. If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. Applicants who are admitted to deemed universities for professional courses are not applicable. Benefits The selected candidates will receive benefits in the following manner:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) 100 % benefit will be given to the students enrolled in D.Ed and B.Ed courses. The fee structure for students in aided or unaided D.Ed and B.Ed courses follows government rates for the same course. Documents Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions A student can apply for the freeship for one educational course at a time. An applicant cannot avail freeship for more than two professional courses. The applicant must be promoted to the next class for scholarship renewal. The applicants pursuing professional courses admitted for the year 2015-16 onwards who fail the examination two or more times in a particular academic course will not be eligible for the freeship. If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level. Applicants changing their professional/non-professional course mid-academic year will not be eligible for freeship/scholarship for the new course. Scholarships/freeships will continue until the applicant completes one course (e.g., Class 11, 12, Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., Ph.D.). However, if an applicant completes both B.A. and B.Ed. courses and later take admission for M.A., they will not be eligible for scholarship/freeship. But after admission to M.B.A. after B.Ed, they can be eligible for scholarship/freeship as it is a professional postgraduate course. If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim. eligibility_full: SBC students studying in Class 11 and above Region India Award A monthly maintenance allowance up to ₹425 and other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships postmatric-scholarship-to-sbc-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Post Matric Scholarship to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra for the Special Backward Classes (SBC) category students pursuing post-matric studies. The scholarship aims to promote higher education and facilitate the integration of students into the mainstream educational system. The selected students will receive a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹425 and other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Special Backward Classes (SBC) category be pursuing a government-approved course at the post-matric level have obtained admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) round (for students pursuing professional courses) have 75% attendance for the current year have an annual family income less than or equal to ₹1,50,000 Benefits The selected candidates will receive the scholarship as follows:- Course Group Hostellers (in ₹, per month for 10 months) Day Scholars (in ₹, per month for 10 months) Group A 425 190 Group B 290 190 Group C 290 190 Group D 230 120 Group E 150 90 Note:- The scholarship amount will be given to the students from the admission date to till exam completion date. The students will receive 100% of the tuition fee, exam fee, and maintenance allowance to study in government/government-aided/non-aided institutions in professional and non-professional courses. If the applicant takes an admission on or before the 20th of any month, the maintenance allowance will be issued for that current month. Otherwise, maintenance allowance will be entitled from next month. 100% benefit (tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance) is applicable for D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses. For students studying in government-aided /non-aided D.Ed. and B.Ed courses, the fee structure is applicable as per government rates for the same course. Maintenance allowance and exam fees are disbursed to the applicant upon promotion to the next higher class.
documents: Caste certificate issued by a competent authority Income certificate/declaration issued by a competent authority Caste validity certificate Marksheet of HSC or SSC or last examination Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Father/Guardian's death certificate (if applicable) Ration card Leaving certificate Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Note:- Caste validity certificate is mandatory for professional degree courses and professional postgraduate courses. However, it is not mandatory for non-professional courses. A maximum of 2 male children from a family are allowed to avail of this scholarship. For female children, this rule does not apply.
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). selection_criteria: The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi)
contact: Apply Now Eligibility SBC students studying in Class 11 and above Region India Award A monthly maintenance allowance up to ₹425 and other benefits Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships postmatric-scholarship-to-sbc-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Post Matric Scholarship to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra for the Special Backward Classes (SBC) category students pursuing post-matric studies. The students will receive 100% of the tuition fee, exam fee, and maintenance allowance to study in government/government-aided/non-aided institutions in professional and non-professional courses. If the applicant takes an admission on or before the 20th of any month, the maintenance allowance will be issued for that current month. Otherwise, maintenance allowance will be entitled from next month. 100% benefit (tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance) is applicable for D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses. For students studying in government-aided /non-aided D.Ed. and B.Ed courses, the fee structure is applicable as per government rates for the same course. Maintenance allowance and exam fees are disbursed to the applicant upon promotion to the next higher class. Documents Caste certificate issued by a competent authority Income certificate/declaration issued by a competent authority Caste validity certificate Marksheet of HSC or SSC or last examination Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Father/Guardian's death certificate (if applicable) Ration card Leaving certificate Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Note:- Caste validity certificate is mandatory for professional degree courses and professional postgraduate courses. However, it is not mandatory for non-professional courses. A maximum of 2 male children from a family are allowed to avail of this scholarship. For female children, this rule does not apply. How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions The applicants are required to pass the previous year's examination for the renewal of the scholarship. If an applicant fails for a year, no reimbursement is provided for that specific year. If the applicants get admission to a government hostel, they will be eligible for one-third amount of hostlers. Scholarships/freeship will continue until the applicant completes one course. If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level. No scholarship will be paid to the applicants under this scheme who are already receiving a scholarship/stipend from another source. The applicants who change from professional to non-professional courses will not receive any scholarship. If an applicant changes their course in between academic years, they will not be eligible for further assistance. If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim. Scholarships or freeships will continue to be provided to students until they complete one course, such as 11th or 12th grade Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., or Ph.D. If a student completes both B.A. and B.Ed. degrees and later decide to pursue an M.A., they will no longer be eligible for scholarship/freeship benefits. However, if a student opts for an M.B.A. following their B.Ed., they may be eligible for scholarship or freeship support, as it is considered a professional postgraduate course. For Group A, if an applicant pursuing a Group A course fails in the examination for the first time, the award may be renewed. However, for second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class. For Group B, C, D, and E, the applicant must secure a promotion to a higher class. In case of second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class. Contact Details VJNT, OBC and SBC Welfare Department, Maharashtra Annex Building-139, First Floor, Mantralaya Madam Cama Road, Hutatma Rajguru Chawk Mumbai 400032 Phone Number: 18001208040 Source : https://mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

title: Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to OBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: Up to 100% reimbursement in tuition, exams, and other admissible fees
eligibility: OBC students studying in Class 11 and above
description: Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to OBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government to Maharashtra for Other Backward Classes (OBC) students pursuing post-matric education (Class 11 and above). Selected scholars will receive up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees.
eligibility_full: OBC students studying in Class 11 and above Region India Award Up to 100% reimbursement in tuition, exams, and other admissible fees Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Discover more Education Educational Resources Primary & Secondary Schooling (K-12) Home Scholarships tuition-fees-and-examination-fees-to-obc-students-maharashtra-2025-26 About The Program Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to OBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government to Maharashtra for Other Backward Classes (OBC) students pursuing post-matric education (Class 11 and above). Selected scholars will receive up to 100% reimbursement for tuition, exams, and other admissible fees. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Other Backward Classes (OBC) category be pursuing studies at the post-matric level (Class 11 and above) in a government/government-aided/private institution have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) process (for professional courses) have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Note:- The applicants must have been admitted to government, government-aided, private non-aided, or private permanently non-aided institutions for professional courses. If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. Applicants who are admitted to deemed universities for professional courses are not eligible.
benefits: Tuition fees and exam fees are reimbursed to eligible candidates under this scholarship scheme. The reimbursement policy is as follows: Government, government-approved private non-aided, and permanently non-granted colleges: Students admitted through the CAP round in professional courses are eligible for a 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees approved by the Education Fee Committee. Government and aided institutions: Students pursuing professional courses in these institutions are eligible for a 100% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees. Non-government and unaided institutions: Students pursuing professional courses in these institutions are eligible for a 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees. OBC applicants in private unaided institutions: For OBC Students pursuing non-professional courses in private unaided institutions, the fee structure of government-aided institutions will be applicable for tuition fees, exam fees, and other fees. Note:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship fees will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate)
documents: Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026
selection_criteria: The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi)

If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. The reimbursement policy is as follows: Government, government-approved private non-aided, and permanently non-granted colleges: Students admitted through the CAP round in professional courses are eligible for a 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees approved by the Education Fee Committee. Government and aided institutions: Students pursuing professional courses in these institutions are eligible for a 100% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees. Non-government and unaided institutions: Students pursuing professional courses in these institutions are eligible for a 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and exam fees. OBC applicants in private unaided institutions: For OBC Students pursuing non-professional courses in private unaided institutions, the fee structure of government-aided institutions will be applicable for tuition fees, exam fees, and other fees. Note:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship fees will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Documents Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card How can you apply? Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions A student can apply for the freeship for one educational course at a time. An applicant cannot avail freeship for more than two professional courses. The applicant must be promoted to the next class for scholarship renewal. The applicants pursuing professional courses admitted for the year 2015-16 onwards who fail the examination two or more times in a particular academic course will not be eligible for the freeship. If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level. Applicants changing their professional/non-professional course mid-academic year will not be eligible for freeship/scholarship for the new course. Scholarships/freeships will continue until the applicant completes one course (e.g., Class 11, 12, Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., Ph.D.). However, if an applicant completes both B.A. and B.Ed. courses and later take admission for M.A., they will not be eligible for scholarship/freeship. But after admission to M.B.A. after B.Ed, they can be eligible for scholarship/freeship as it is a professional postgraduate course. If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim. description: Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, to Special Backwards Classes (SBC) students pursuing studies in Class 11 and above. The scholarship aims to create opportunities for students to pursue higher education. The selected students will receive 100% tuition fees and exam fee reimbursement.
eligibility_full: SBC students in Class 11 and above Region India Award 100% tuition fees and examination fees reimbursement Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Discover more Education Primary & Secondary Schooling (K-12) Educational Resources Home Scholarships tuition-fees-and-examination-fees-to-sbc-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, to Special Backwards Classes (SBC) students pursuing studies in Class 11 and above. The scholarship aims to create opportunities for students to pursue higher education. The selected students will receive 100% tuition fees and exam fee reimbursement. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Special Backwards Classes (SBC) category be pursuing a government-approved course at the post-matric level (Class 11 and above) in a government/government-aided/private institution have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) process (for professional courses) have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Note:- The applicants must have been admitted to government, government-aided, private non-aided, or private permanently non-aided institutions for professional courses. If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. Applicants who are admitted to deemed universities for professional courses are not eligible.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive benefits in the following manner:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) 100 % benefit will be given to the students enrolled in D.Ed. and B.Ed courses. The fee structure for students in aided or unaided D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses follow government rates for the same course.
documents: Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026
selection_criteria: The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (in Marathi)
contact: Apply Now Eligibility SBC students in Class 11 and above Region India Award 100% tuition fees and examination fees reimbursement Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Discover more Education Primary & Secondary Schooling (K-12) Educational Resources Home Scholarships tuition-fees-and-examination-fees-to-sbc-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Tuition Fees and Examination Fees to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra, to Special Backwards Classes (SBC) students pursuing studies in Class 11 and above. The scholarship aims to create opportunities for students to pursue higher education. The selected students will receive 100% tuition fees and exam fee reimbursement. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Special Backwards Classes (SBC) category be pursuing a government-approved course at the post-matric level (Class 11 and above) in a government/government-aided/private institution have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) process (for professional courses) have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Note:- The applicants must have been admitted to government, government-aided, private non-aided, or private permanently non-aided institutions for professional courses. If the applicant is admitted through the Association of Managements of Unaided Private Medical and Dental Colleges entrance examination or through the government common entrance examination, they will be eligible for freeship. Applicants who are admitted to deemed universities for professional courses are not eligible. Benefits The selected candidates will receive benefits in the following manner:- A freeship will be given to the students enrolled in one of the following courses: Health Science(Medical, Dental, Homeopathy, Unani, Ayurveda, Physiotherapy, Business Aid, Nursing) Engineering Pharmacology HMCT (Diploma and Degree) MBA/MMS MCA Scholarship will be given to the students pursuing a diploma/degree in one of the following courses: Agricultural colleges (diploma) Dairy Business Department (diploma) Colleges for Agricultural and Allied subjects (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Bio-Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) Agriculture and Food Technology Colleges (degree and postgraduate) 100 % benefit will be given to the students enrolled in D.Ed. and B.Ed courses. The fee structure for students in aided or unaided D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses follow government rates for the same course. Documents Caste and income certificate issued by a competent authority HSC or SSC marksheet or last examination marksheet Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap) Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries Father's death certificate (if applicable) Caste validity certificate (for professional courses as per GR dated 31st July 2008) CAP Allotment Letter (For professional courses) Leaving certificate Ration card How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (in Marathi) Terms and Conditions A student can apply for the freeship for one educational course at a time. An applicant cannot avail of freeship for more than two professional courses. The applicant must be promoted to the next class for scholarship renewal. The applicants pursuing professional courses admitted for the year 2015-16 onwards who fail the examination two or more times in a particular academic course will not be eligible for the freeship. If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level. Applicants changing their professional/non-professional course mid-academic year will not be eligible for freeship/scholarship for the new course. Scholarships/freeships will continue until the applicant completes one course (e.g., Class 11, 12, Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., Ph.D.). However, if an applicant completes both B.A. and B.Ed. courses and later take admission for M.A., they will not be eligible for scholarship/freeship. But after admission to M.B.A. after B.Ed, they can be eligible for scholarship/freeship as it is a professional postgraduate course. If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim. Contact Details VJNT, OBC and SBC Welfare Department, Maharashtra Annex Building-139, First Floor, Mantralaya Madam Cama Road, Hutatma Rajguru Chawk Mumbai 400032 Phone Number: 18001208040 Source : https://mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

description: The Rajarshri Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Fee Reimbursement Scholarship Scheme, Maharashtra 2025-26, has been launched by the Maharashtra State Government to assist economically weaker section (EWS) students in pursuing higher education. The primary objective of this scheme is to enable EBC students to enrol in professional courses like MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS, BPTh, BOT, B.Sc Nursing, BUMS, BP&O, and BASLP. The scheme provides up to 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and development charges to students.

title: Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Hostel Maintenance Allowance Scheme, Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: Hostel maintenance allowance up to ₹30,000 per annum
eligibility: Wards of alpabhudharak shetkari/registered labourers pursuing specified medical degree courses
description: Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Hostel Maintenance Allowance Scheme, Maharashtra 2024-25 is an initiative of the Directorate of Medical Education Research, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to students pursuing specified medical courses in government-aided/corporation/private unaided colleges. Selected students will receive a hostel maintenance allowance of up to ₹30,000 per annum.
eligibility_full: Wards of alpabhudharak shetkari/registered labourers pursuing specified medical degree courses Region India Award Hostel maintenance allowance up to ₹30,000 per annum Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships dr-panjabrao-deshmukh-hostel-maintenance-allowance-scheme-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Hostel Maintenance Allowance Scheme, Maharashtra 2024-25 is an initiative of the Directorate of Medical Education Research, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to students pursuing specified medical courses in government-aided/corporation/private unaided colleges. Selected students will receive a hostel maintenance allowance of up to ₹30,000 per annum. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be a permanent resident of Maharashtra be a child of a registered labourer and/or Alpabhudharak shetkari (marginal landholders) have applied for a degree course in one of the following disciplines in a government-aided, corporation, or private unaided college: Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) Bachelor of Homoeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS) Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPTH) B.Sc. Nursing Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery (BUMS) Bachelor's in Prosthetics and Orthotics (BPO) Bachelor's in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (BASLP) Dual-Degree programs have taken admission in hostels located in major cities of Maharashtra like Mumbai, Pune, Aurangabad, Nagpur, or other locations have an annual family income of less than ₹8,00,000 Note:- Candidates are eligible if they have been admitted under the General or SEBC category.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive the hostel maintenance allowances as follows: Category Students with an annual family income of less than ₹1,00,000 (for a duration of 10 months) Students whose parents are registered labourers/Alpabhudharak shetkari (marginal landholders) (for a duration of 10 months) For students studying in Mumbai, Pune, Nagpur, and Aurangabad ₹3,000 ₹30,000 For other locations in Maharashtra ₹2,000 ₹20,000
documents: Domicile certificate HSC and SSC mark sheets (for new applicants) Income certificate of parent(s) Previous year's mark sheet Affidavit Aadhaar card Father's PAN card Student's PAN card (optional) Mother's PAN card (optional) Registered Laborers/Alapbhudarak Shikari must submit a proof letter approved by the tahsildar or an income certificate of parents

selection_criteria: The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (in Marathi)

Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the open category have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) be pursuing MBBS, BDS, postgraduate medical (MD/MS) in private un-aided colleges
benefits: The selected students will receive a reimbursement of the institute's Fees Regulatory Authority (FRA) approved fees for private institutes and government-approved fees for government institutes.

The selected students will receive reimbursement of the institute's FRA-approved fees. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the open category have taken admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP) be pursuing MBBS, BDS, postgraduate medical (MD/MS) in private un-aided colleges Benefits The selected students will receive a reimbursement of the institute's Fees Regulatory Authority (FRA) approved fees for private institutes and government-approved fees for government institutes. Documents Aadhaar card Domicile certificate HSC and SSC marksheet Previous year's marksheet Father's PAN card Student's PAN card (optional) Mother's PAN card (optional) How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions There is no income criteria check for this scheme. Renewal of the scheme requires a minimum of 50% attendance. Students with Allowed to Keep Terms (ATKT) will be eligible for renewal. Deemed university students are not eligible for this scheme. Candidates admitted through the management quota are not eligible for this scholarship. The applicant should not have a gap of two years or more in the course duration. If a candidate fails in the academic year, does not fulfill attendance criteria, or is found guilty of misconduct, they will not be eligible for the scheme. eligibility: Socially and Economically Backward Classes (SEBC) and Open category students
description: Vocational Training Fee Reimbursement Scholarship for Socially and Economically Backwards Category (SEBC) and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Skill Development, Employment and Entrepreneurship Department, Government of Maharashtra. This scholarship offers financial assistance to eligible SEBC and EWS students, covering up to 100% of their course fees.
eligibility_full: Socially and Economically Backward Classes (SEBC) and Open category students Region India Award Up to 100% Course Fee Reimbursement Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Discover more Primary & Secondary Schooling (K-12) Education Home Scholarships vocational-training-fee-reimbursement-scholarship-for-sebc-and-ews-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Vocational Training Fee Reimbursement Scholarship for Socially and Economically Backwards Category (SEBC) and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Skill Development, Employment and Entrepreneurship Department, Government of Maharashtra. This scholarship offers financial assistance to eligible SEBC and EWS students, covering up to 100% of their course fees. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Socially and Economically Backwards Class (SEBC) and the Open category (Economically Weaker Section - EWS) have been admitted through the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) scheme in Government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) or Private ITIs have been admitted through the Central Online Admission Process have been admitted to a course approved by the Directorate General of Training (DGT), New Delhi or the Maharashtra State Council for Vocational Training (MSCVT) not have availed of any benefit for a course previously from a Government or Private ITI not have availed any benefit from training programs sponsored by the State/Central Government/Department/Local Body/Company or Corporation have an annual family income of more than ₹2,50,000 but less than or equal to ₹8,00,000
benefits: Selected candidates will receive the following benefits:- Candidates with a total family income between ₹2,50,000 and ₹8,00,000 will receive an 80% reimbursement of their private/government institute course fee. Candidates with a total family income of ₹2,50,000 or less will receive a 100% reimbursement of their private/government institute course fee.

Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to the Socially and Economically Backwards Class (SEBC) and the Open category (Economically Weaker Section - EWS) have been admitted through the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) scheme in Government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) or Private ITIs have been admitted through the Central Online Admission Process have been admitted to a course approved by the Directorate General of Training (DGT), New Delhi or the Maharashtra State Council for Vocational Training (MSCVT) not have availed of any benefit for a course previously from a Government or Private ITI not have availed any benefit from training programs sponsored by the State/Central Government/Department/Local Body/Company or Corporation have an annual family income of more than ₹2,50,000 but less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Benefits Selected candidates will receive the following benefits:- Candidates with a total family income between ₹2,50,000 and ₹8,00,000 will receive an 80% reimbursement of their private/government institute course fee. Selected students must adhere to the renewal policy and submit an annual report of their academic progress to renew the scholarship. Students must maintain satisfactory attendance and academic progress. Failure to do so may result in scholarship termination. Candidates must attend every semester/exam. It provides financial aid to students who are wards of registered labourers or marginal farmers and have secured admission to technical and professional courses in government, government-funded, or non-affiliated colleges/polytechnics. Selected students will receive financial assistance of up to ₹30,000 for a period of ten months.
eligibility_full: Children of registered laborers and marginal farmers Region India Award Up to ₹30,000 for 10 months Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships dte-dr-punjabrao-deshmukh-vasatigruh-nirvah-bhatta-yojna-maharashtra-2025 About The Program DTE Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Vasatigruh Nirvah Bhatta Yojna, Maharashtra 2024-25 is an initiative of the Department of Technical Education, Government of Maharashtra. It provides financial aid to students who are wards of registered labourers or marginal farmers and have secured admission to technical and professional courses in government, government-funded, or non-affiliated colleges/polytechnics. Selected students will receive financial assistance of up to ₹30,000 for a period of ten months. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be a domicile of Maharashtra be a child of registered labour or a child of Alpabhudharak (Marginal Land Holder) be admitted to professional and technical courses, including diploma/graduation/postgraduation degree as mentioned in the Government Resolution (GR) hold a bonafide certificate from the respective institution have secured a minimum of 50% attendance in the previous semester (except for those freshly admitted to college) have an annual family income of less than ₹8,00,000
benefits: Selected students will receive the following benefits: Child of registered labour/alpabhudharak (Marginal Land Holder): For students studying in an institute located in MMRDA/PMRDA/Aurangabad City/Nagpur City: ₹30,000 for 10 months For students studying in an institute located in other areas: ₹20,000 for 10 months For students having an annual family income of up to ₹8,00,000: For students studying in an institute located in MMRDA/PMRDA/Aurangabad City/Nagpur City: ₹10,000 for 10 months For students studying in an institute located in other areas: ₹8,000 for 10 months

eligibility_full: Class 11 or 12 students Region India Award Up to ₹160 per month for 10 months Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships merit-scholarships-for-ebc-students-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Open Merit Scholarships for EBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the School Education and Sports Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to Economically Backwards Classes (EBC) category students studying in Class 11 or 12. description: MCAER Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Shikshan Shulkh Shishyavrutti Yojna (EBC) Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Maharashtra Council Of Agriculture Education And Research (MCAER), Government of Maharashtra to provide financial assistance to EBC (Economic Backward Class) students enrolled in a professional, non-professional and technical diploma/degree/postgraduate courses. A 50% reimbursement of tuition and examination fees will be provided to selected students.
eligibility_full: Students enrolled in UG/PG/diploma courses Region India Award A reimbursement of 100% of tuition fees and 100% of exam fees Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships mcaer-rajarshi-chhatrapati-shahu-maharaj-shikshan-shulkh-shishyavrutti-yojna-ebc-students-maharashtra-2025-26 About The Program MCAER Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Shikshan Shulkh Shishyavrutti Yojna (EBC) Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Maharashtra Council Of Agriculture Education And Research (MCAER), Government of Maharashtra to provide financial assistance to EBC (Economic Backward Class) students enrolled in a professional, non-professional and technical diploma/degree/postgraduate courses. A 50% reimbursement of tuition and examination fees will be provided to selected students. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be a domicile of Maharashtra have been admitted to a professional, non-professional, or technical course (diploma, graduation, or postgraduate degree) in a recognized institution through the Centralized Admission Process (CAP) have been admitted under the General or Socially and Economically Backward Classes (SEBC) category have an annual family income of no more than ₹8,00,000
benefits: Selected students will receive a 50% tuition and exam fee reimbursement
documents: Marksheet for Class 10 (SSC) and above Domicile certificate Income certificate CAP-related documents Undertaking stating that no more than two beneficiaries from the family are availing the scholarship Proof of biometric attendance (Interface UIDAI)

contact: Apply Now Eligibility Students enrolled in UG/PG/diploma courses Region India Award A reimbursement of 100% of tuition fees and 100% of exam fees Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships mcaer-rajarshi-chhatrapati-shahu-maharaj-shikshan-shulkh-shishyavrutti-yojna-ebc-students-maharashtra-2025-26 About The Program MCAER Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Shikshan Shulkh Shishyavrutti Yojna (EBC) Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Maharashtra Council Of Agriculture Education And Research (MCAER), Government of Maharashtra to provide financial assistance to EBC (Economic Backward Class) students enrolled in a professional, non-professional and technical diploma/degree/postgraduate courses. A 50% reimbursement of tuition and examination fees will be provided to selected students. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be a domicile of Maharashtra have been admitted to a professional, non-professional, or technical course (diploma, graduation, or postgraduate degree) in a recognized institution through the Centralized Admission Process (CAP) have been admitted under the General or Socially and Economically Backward Classes (SEBC) category have an annual family income of no more than ₹8,00,000 Benefits Selected students will receive a 50% tuition and exam fee reimbursement Documents Marksheet for Class 10 (SSC) and above Domicile certificate Income certificate CAP-related documents Undertaking stating that no more than two beneficiaries from the family are availing the scholarship Proof of biometric attendance (Interface UIDAI) How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions Only two children from the same family are eligible for the scheme. The total annual income of the family or guardian must not exceed ₹8 lakhs. Students must have a minimum of 50% attendance in the previous semester (except for newly admitted students). There must be no gap of two or more years in the student's academic record during the course. Candidates are eligible only if they have been admitted under the General or SEBC category. Contact Details Maharashtra Council Of Agriculture Education And Research (MCAER), Government of Maharashtra 132/B, Bhamburda, Bhosale Nagar, Pune - 411007 Email ID: mcaer@rediffmail.com Administration Department: (020) 25528208 (Ext. 137) Education Department: (020) 25528688 (Ext. 125) Research Department: (020) 25528688 (Ext. 145) Source : https://www.mcaer.org/

title: State Minority Scholarship for Pursuing Higher Professional Education Scholarship (Part I), Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: Up to ₹50,000 per annum or tuition and exam fees (whichever is less)
eligibility: Minority students pursuing professional or technical courses
description: State Minority Scholarship for Pursuing Higher Professional Education Scholarship (Part I) 2025-26 is an initiative of the Minority Development Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial aid to students belonging to religious minority communities for pursuing professional or technical degree/diploma courses. The selected students will receive up to ₹50,000 per annum or actual tuition and exam fees (whichever is less).
eligibility_full: Minority students pursuing professional or technical courses Region India Award Up to ₹50,000 per annum or tuition and exam fees (whichever is less) Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Discover more Educational Resources Primary & Secondary Schooling (K-12) Home Scholarships state-minority-scholarship-for-pursuing-higher-professional-education-scholarship-part-i-maharashtra-2025 About The Program State Minority Scholarship for Pursuing Higher Professional Education Scholarship (Part I) 2025-26 is an initiative of the Minority Development Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial aid to students belonging to religious minority communities for pursuing professional or technical degree/diploma courses. The selected students will receive up to ₹50,000 per annum or actual tuition and exam fees (whichever is less). Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to minority communities including Muslim, Buddhist, Christian, Sikh, Parsi, Jain, and Jewish have passed SSC from Maharashtra have taken admission in professional or technical diploma/degree courses hold a bonafide certificate from the respective institution have got admission through the Centralised Admission Process (CAP)/Institute Level have an annual family income not exceeding ₹8,00,000
benefits: The selected students will receive a maximum scholarship amount of ₹50,000 per annum or actual tuition fees, whichever is less.
documents: Domicile certificate Marksheet of the previous examination Residence proof such as Aadhaar card, Voter ID, Electricity bill, etc. Educational certificate - SSC (Class 10) and onwards Income and Minority declaration - Affidavit on Non-Judicial Stamp Paper or Income certificate from the employer, if any Note:- A school leaving certificate can also be considered as Proof of Minority or Self Declaration.

title: Scholarship for Minority Students Pursuing Higher and Professional Courses (DMER), Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: Total annual course fees or ₹50,000 (whichever is less)
eligibility: Students pursuing specified medical courses
description: The Scholarship for Minority Students Pursuing Higher and Professional Courses (DMER), Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Minority Development Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to minority students pursuing specified health science courses. Selected students will receive a scholarship amount of either the total annual course fee or ₹50,000, whichever is less.
eligibility_full: Students pursuing specified medical courses Region India Award Total annual course fees or ₹50,000 (whichever is less) Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships scholarship-for-minority-students-pursuing-higher-and-professional-courses-dmer-maharashtra-2025 About The Program The Scholarship for Minority Students Pursuing Higher and Professional Courses (DMER), Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Minority Development Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to minority students pursuing specified health science courses. Selected students will receive a scholarship amount of either the total annual course fee or ₹50,000, whichever is less. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to minority communities, including Muslim, Buddhist, Christian, Jain, Sikh, Parsi, and Jewish have taken admission to a course through CET/competitive exam or based on marks obtained in Higher Secondary School (HSC) have taken admission in one of the following courses: Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) Bachelor of Homoeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS) Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery (BUMS) Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPTH) Bachelor of Occupational Therapy (BOTH) Bachelor's in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (BASLP) Bachelor in Prosthetics and Orthotics (BPO) Bachelor of Paramedical Technology (BPMT) B.Sc./M.Sc. Nursing Ophthalmic Assistant Optometry Post Basic B.Sc Nursing Courses affiliated with Maharashtra University of Health Science, Nashik have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000
benefits: The selected students will receive a scholarship amount of either the total annual course fee or ₹50,000, whichever is less.
documents: Aadhaar card Domicile Certificate School Leaving Certificate HSC and SSC marksheets Previous year's marksheets Father’s PAN card Student’s PAN card (optional) Mother’s PAN card (optional) Income certificate of parents/Form no.16 If the applicant is studying outside Maharashtra, the following documents are required to be submitted: Copy of Fees Regulatory Authority (FRA) Bonafide for the current academic year Letter from concerned council/authority stating the institute is recognised Note:- The income certificate/affidavit should explicitly mention that the candidate belongs to a minority community.

contact: Apply Now Eligibility Students pursuing specified medical courses Region India Award Total annual course fees or ₹50,000 (whichever is less) Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships scholarship-for-minority-students-pursuing-higher-and-professional-courses-dmer-maharashtra-2025 About The Program The Scholarship for Minority Students Pursuing Higher and Professional Courses (DMER), Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Minority Development Department, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to minority students pursuing specified health science courses. Selected students will receive a scholarship amount of either the total annual course fee or ₹50,000, whichever is less. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian citizen be a domicile of Maharashtra belong to minority communities, including Muslim, Buddhist, Christian, Jain, Sikh, Parsi, and Jewish have taken admission to a course through CET/competitive exam or based on marks obtained in Higher Secondary School (HSC) have taken admission in one of the following courses: Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) Bachelor of Homoeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS) Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery (BUMS) Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPTH) Bachelor of Occupational Therapy (BOTH) Bachelor's in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (BASLP) Bachelor in Prosthetics and Orthotics (BPO) Bachelor of Paramedical Technology (BPMT) B.Sc./M.Sc. Nursing Ophthalmic Assistant Optometry Post Basic B.Sc Nursing Courses affiliated with Maharashtra University of Health Science, Nashik have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 Benefits The selected students will receive a scholarship amount of either the total annual course fee or ₹50,000, whichever is less. Documents Aadhaar card Domicile Certificate School Leaving Certificate HSC and SSC marksheets Previous year's marksheets Father’s PAN card Student’s PAN card (optional) Mother’s PAN card (optional) Income certificate of parents/Form no.16 If the applicant is studying outside Maharashtra, the following documents are required to be submitted: Copy of Fees Regulatory Authority (FRA) Bonafide for the current academic year Letter from concerned council/authority stating the institute is recognised Note:- The income certificate/affidavit should explicitly mention that the candidate belongs to a minority community. How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions 30% of seats are reserved for girls. If the targeted scholarship amount for a particular minority community is not achieved, scholarships for students from other minority communities can be included. Students pursuing their education outside of Maharashtra are eligible for the scholarship, provided they are domiciled in Maharashtra or have resided in Maharashtra for at least 15 years. Contact Details Minority Development Department 5th & 7th Floor, Mantralaya Extension Building Hutatma Rajaguru Chowk Madam Kema Marg, Mumbai - 40032 Email ID: desk1.end-mah@gov.in | Phone Number: (022) 22830626 Source : https://mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

Selected students can receive up to ₹30,000 for 10 months. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be a domicile of Maharashtra be the child of a registered labourer or Alpabhudarak (marginal landholder) be enrolled in a professional course at a government, non-government aided, partially aided-unaided, or permanently unaided college, or a non-agricultural university or sub-centre (excluding private or self-financed private universities) under the Directorate of Art Department have taken admission under the general category be a hosteller (staying in a government/private hostel, paying guest accommodation, or as a tenant) have an annual family income of less than ₹8,00,000 attempt every semester or annual exam Non-Eligibility: Applicants must not be availing of any other Nirvah Bhatta benefits. Students should not have a gap of more than 2 years during the course. Benefits The selected candidates will receive the following benefits for 10 months:- Category Allowances Students pursuing studies in Mumbai suburbs, Navi Mumbai, Thane, Pune, Pimpri Chinchwad and Nagpur Students pursuing studies in other revenue divisional cities and remaining C-class municipal areas Students pursuing studies in other districts Students pursuing studies in Talukas For students of Registered Labour/Alpabhudharak (Marginal Land Holder) (in ₹ for 10 months) Food allowance 32,000 28,000 25,000 23,000 For students having an annual family income of up to ₹1,00,000 (in ₹ for 10 months) Residence allowance 20,000 15,000 12,000 10,000 For students having an annual family income of between ₹1,00,000-₹8,00,000 (in ₹ for 10 months) Subsistence allowance 8,000 8,000 6,000 5,000 Total 60,000 51,000 43,000 38,000 Documents Domicile certificate Registered labourer certificate/Alpabudharak certificate/Family annual income certificate Hosteller document CAP-related document Attendance certificate Previous year's marksheet Gap-related document (if applicable) Previous year's annual income certificate Family declaration certificate for two children How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it. Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the 'Register' button. Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard. Step 6: Log in using the username and password. Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Selection Criteria The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Important documents Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi) Terms and Conditions 33% of the seats are reserved for girls. The Government Resolution states that the scheme is only available to the first two children of a family. Students who benefited from the scholarship last year are eligible to apply for renewal. To renew the application, students must use their assigned application ID. The published list is available on the Department of Art's website. Contact Details Director of Art, Government of Maharashtra 411, Fourth Floor, Mantralaya Annex, Madam Cama Road, Nariman Point, Mumbai - 400032 Phone Number: (022)-22025301 Source: https://mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in/SchemeData/SchemeData?str=E9DDFA703C38E51A78ED7CE6EFC1BA66278D9242EB6BD70623E4341DD9200A3E
