title: Government of India Post-Matric Scholarship, Maharashtra 2025-26
amount: A monthly maintenance allowance up to ₹1,200 and other benefits
eligibility: Students in Class 11 and above
description: Government of India Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra, to support SC/Neo-Buddhist students pursuing studies beyond Class 10 within the state. This scholarship aims to provide financial assistance to students and reduce dropout rates in educational institutions. Selected students will receive a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹1,200 and other
eligibility_full: Students in Class 11 and above Region India Award A monthly maintenance allowance up to ₹1,200 and other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships government-of-india-postmatric-scholarship-maharashtra-2025 About The Program Government of India Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an initiative of the Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra, to support SC/Neo-Buddhist students pursuing studies beyond Class 10 within the state. This scholarship aims to provide financial assistance to students and reduce dropout rates in educational institutions. title: Inlaks Shivdasani Scholarships 2026
amount: A maximum of $1,20,000 and other benefits (one-time)
eligibility: Indian students pursuing PG degree abroad
description: Inlaks Shivdasani Scholarships 2026 is a merit-based opportunity offered by the Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation (a non-profit organisation), to Indian students pursuing postgraduate studies at European, American, and UK universities abroad. The selected students will receive a maximum of $1,20,000 and orher
eligibility_full: Indian students pursuing PG degree abroad Region Abroad Award A maximum of $1,20,000 and other
benefits: (one-time) Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships inlaks-shivdasani-scholarships-2026 About The Program Inlaks Shivdasani Scholarships 2026 is a merit-based opportunity offered by the Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation (a non-profit organisation), to Indian students pursuing postgraduate studies at European, American, and UK universities abroad. The selected students will receive a maximum of $1,20,000 and orher benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, the applicants must:- be an Indian passport holder who is residing in India at the time of application born on or after 1st January 1996 hold a first-class degree or equivalent from a recognised university/institution in India fulfill the below-mentioned academic criteria: Social Sciences, Humanities, Law, Fine Arts, Architecture, and related subjects: a minimum academic grade of 65%, CGPA 6.8/10, or GPA 2.6/4 Mathematics, Sciences, Environment, and related subjects: a minimum academic grade of 70%, CGPA 7.2/10, or GPA 2.8/4 hold an English language certification as a conditional part of their offer letter have received a valid deferred offer letter for the academic year 2026-27 Note:- Candidates having a postgraduate qualification (e.g. Master’s or Ph.D.) from an institution abroad are not eligible to apply. Candidates who are already studying or have started their postgraduate education at an institution abroad are not eligible to apply. Applicants who hold an undergraduate degree from a recognised foreign university, with two years of continuous residence, employment, or study in India after graduation are eligible. Benefits Selected students will receive a one-time support of $1,20,000 along with a travel allowance for a one-way ticket in addition to the tuition, maintenance, and health insurance. Note:- The foundation will not provide any scholarships for visas, travel, and health insurance in isolation. If the course costs more than the scholarship amount, applicants must demonstrate financial self-sufficiency for the remaining expenses at the time of application.
documents: Academic transcripts, including degrees, certificates, marksheets, etc. Course-related portfolio/links/writing samples Updated Resume/Curriculum Vitae (CV) Photograph (in JPEG or PNG format) TOEFL/IELTS/GRE score sheets (if applicable) Extracurricular attainments (if any) Fee statement as below: If the tuition fees are mentioned in the offer letter, then highlight the fee and upload the letter in the fee statement section as a PDF File format document. If the tuition fee is not mentioned in the offer letter, then from the university website, download the tuition document, highlight the fees of the course, and upload it as a PDF file format. Proof of additional funding such as personal bank statements/parent(s)/relative bank statements, and/or investment with a letter of authority/personal investment documents/approval letter of an educational loan, etc. (Note:- No property documents are allowed.) Passport (Note:- If expired, continue to upload the old passport along with the receipt of the application made for a new one as one PDF file document.) Admission/offer letter (Note:- If the applicant has not received an admission/offer letter by 22nd March 2024, an acknowledgment of the application to the said University can be uploaded in a PDF file format.) 2 academic references (for students), and one professional one for those who are working (Note:- The reference letter must be on a letterhead.)
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship by following the below-mentioned steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.) Step 3: Read the details carefully and click on the 'FOR APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CLICK HERE' button. Step 4: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents in the prescribed format, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Closing Date: 31st March 2026 (12 PM IST)
selection_criteria: The selection process consists of the following three stages:- review of eligible applications online preliminary interviews with candidates chosen from the review a final in-person interview with those who succeed in the preliminary interview Applicants will be assessed based on their past, and present achievements, and future potential. Candidates applying for scholarships in art and design (fine/performing arts) will be primarily assessed on their portfolios. The decision made by the Inlaks Selection Committee will be considered final. Note:- Candidates who do not receive any communication from the Foundation by 19th April 2024 must assume that their applications have not been successful.

Benefits Selected students will receive a one-time support of $1,20,000 along with a travel allowance for a one-way ticket in addition to the tuition, maintenance, and health insurance. Note:- The foundation will not provide any scholarships for visas, travel, and health insurance in isolation. If the course costs more than the scholarship amount, applicants must demonstrate financial self-sufficiency for the remaining expenses at the time of application. Documents Academic transcripts, including degrees, certificates, marksheets, etc. Course-related portfolio/links/writing samples Updated Resume/Curriculum Vitae (CV) Photograph (in JPEG or PNG format) TOEFL/IELTS/GRE score sheets (if applicable) Extracurricular attainments (if any) Fee statement as below: If the tuition fees are mentioned in the offer letter, then highlight the fee and upload the letter in the fee statement section as a PDF File format document. If the tuition fee is not mentioned in the offer letter, then from the university website, download the tuition document, highlight the fees of the course, and upload it as a PDF file format. Proof of additional funding such as personal bank statements/parent(s)/relative bank statements, and/or investment with a letter of authority/personal investment documents/approval letter of an educational loan, etc. (Note:- No property documents are allowed.) Passport (Note:- If expired, continue to upload the old passport along with the receipt of the application made for a new one as one PDF file document.) Admission/offer letter (Note:- If the applicant has not received an admission/offer letter by 22nd March 2024, an acknowledgment of the application to the said University can be uploaded in a PDF file format.) 2 academic references (for students), and one professional one for those who are working (Note:- The reference letter must be on a letterhead.) How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship by following the below-mentioned steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.) Step 3: Read the details carefully and click on the 'FOR APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CLICK HERE' button. Step 4: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents in the prescribed format, and submit. Apply Now Important Dates Application Closing Date: 31st March 2026 (12 PM IST) Selection Criteria The selection process consists of the following three stages:- review of eligible applications online preliminary interviews with candidates chosen from the review a final in-person interview with those who succeed in the preliminary interview Applicants will be assessed based on their past, and present achievements, and future potential. Candidates applying for scholarships in art and design (fine/performing arts) will be primarily assessed on their portfolios. The decision made by the Inlaks Selection Committee will be considered final. Note:- Candidates who do not receive any communication from the Foundation by 19th April 2024 must assume that their applications have not been successful. Terms and Conditions Only one submission per candidate will be accepted. Incomplete applications will not be accepted. If the scholarship does not commence within nine months of award, it will be forfeited. Students applying to specific universities may receive additional funding. These universities include: Imperial College London The Royal College of Art (RCA) London The University of Cambridge (Cambridge Trust) Sciences Po Paris King's College London Hertie School Berlin The foundation offers joint scholarships with these institutions, potentially exceeding the USD 1,20,000 limit, to eligible applicants. Recipients of the Foundation's award may also pursue other funding sources, such as teaching assistantships or part-time jobs. However, they are required to inform the Foundation of any additional funding they are receiving. Fieldwork in India during the program is not permitted for scholarship applicants, except for PhD students in Social Sciences, Public Policy, Arts & Humanities applying under the King's College London collaboration. The foundation gives scholarships in a variety of subjects but DOES NOT fund the following courses: Business and Finance Computer Science Public Health Fashion Design Hospitality and Tourism Management Studies (i.e. MBA) Medicine, Dentistry, and related therapies Engineering (except Engineering and Natural Sciences at Imperial College, London) Film and Film Animation (except Documentary filmmaking) Indian Studies without Contemporary Relevance Music (except Western Classical singing) Contact Details Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation Email ID: applications@inlaksfoundation.org Source: https://inlaksfoundation.org/opportunities/

title: WINGS — Women INspiring Growth in STEM Scholarship Program
amount: Covers tuition fees of the students (INR 2 lakh p.a)
eligibility: For female undergraduate students enrolled at IIT Bombay
description: IIT Bombay’s Distinguished Alumnus Bharat Desai and his wife, Neerja Sethi, have launched a new scholarship initiative at IIT Bombay for women in STEM, named WINGS — Women INspiring Growth in STEM Scholarship Program. This initiative is aimed at breaking down financial barriers and championing women’s education in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. About WINGS This initiative, in collaboration with IIT Bombay and IITBHF (IIT Bombay Heritage Foundation), aims to expand access to and improve the quality of educational opportunities for female students in India. It believes that a world-class education from IIT Bombay will prepare female students to become future leaders. The Program is intended to cover tuition expenses for female undergraduate students of IIT Bombay, beginning in the 2026–2027 academic year, excluding those who qualify for other government tuition grants or scholarship programs. WINGS — WOMEN INSPIRING GROWTH IN STEM SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM
eligibility_full: Female undergraduate students enrolled at IIT Bombay. Students not availing any other government tuition grant or scholarship. The scholarship will continue for students maintaining a CPI of 6.0 or higher.
benefits: The scholarship covers tuition fees (Rs 2 lakhs per annum) for women students meeting the above eligibility criteria.
documents: No documents are required at this stage.

title: Arturo Falaschi PhD Fellowships ICGEB 2026-27
amount: A monthly stipend of US$ 1,122 (approx. ₹97,000) and other benefits
eligibility: M.Sc. degree holders
description: Arturo Falaschi PhD Fellowships ICGEB, New Delhi 2026-27 is an opportunity offered by the International Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (ICGEB) to highly motivated M.Sc. degree holders. The aim of this fellowship programme is to provide an opportunity for the fellows to obtain the “Doctor Philosophiae” (PhD) degree in the life sciences. The title can be awarded through a number of academic institutions of international standing, which include the Jawaharlal Nehru University in New Delhi. The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of US$ 1,122 (approximately ₹97,000) and other
eligibility_full: M.Sc. degree holders Region India Award A monthly stipend of US$ 1,122 (approx. ₹97,000) and other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships arturo-falaschi-phd-fellowships-icgeb-2026 About The Program Arturo Falaschi PhD Fellowships ICGEB, New Delhi 2026-27 is an opportunity offered by the International Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (ICGEB) to highly motivated M.Sc. degree holders. The aim of this fellowship programme is to provide an opportunity for the fellows to obtain the “Doctor Philosophiae” (PhD) degree in the life sciences. The title can be awarded through a number of academic institutions of international standing, which include the Jawaharlal Nehru University in New Delhi. The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of US$ 1,122 (approximately ₹97,000) and other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, the applicant must - be a national of an ICGEB member state (including India) hold an M.Sc. degree to pursue research at ICGEB Campus, New Delhi have a good working knowledge of the English language, supported by a proficiency certificate (TOEFL, Cambridge certificate or equivalent) [Note:- Not required when scholastic education has been undertaken in English] Note:- There is no age limit for applications for ICGEB New Delhi.. Benefits The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of US$ 1,122 (approximately ₹97,000). Other Benefits:- Travel expenses from the applicant's home country to the ICGEB lab at the beginning of the fellowship and a return travel provision upon completion of the fellowship Medical health insurance coverage Reimbursed visa/permit of stay application and renewal costs Accommodation support Note:- ICGEB makes no financial provision, nor can it provide administrative support for family members of participants in the programme.
documents: Curriculum Vitae (CV) English Language Certificate (TOEFL/Cambridge etc.) [Note:- This document is mandatory for Trieste and is not requested when scholastic education has been undertaken in English.] Academic records (diploma certificates and transcripts giving detailed information on all courses/final examinations completed, credit value or hours per course and grades received Written Statement from the ICGEB Group Leader A valid identification document (passport or identity card) [Note:- Recommended passport validity should be 12 months at least]

The selected candidates will receive €2,300 per month with other
eligibility_full: Science & Engineering scholars Region Germany Award €2,300 monthly with other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships paired-early-career-fellowship-in-applied-research-pecfar-2026 About The Program Paired Early Career Fellowship in Applied Research (PECFAR) 2026 is an opportunity offered by the Indo-German Science & Technology Centre (IGSTC) for early-career researchers from India and Germany. It provides networking and exploration of avenues for collaboration and innovation in Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics (STEM) for Indian scholars. The selected candidates will receive €2,300 per month with other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an Indian pursuing studies from Indian institutions not exceed 40 years of age hold a Master’s degree in Science or a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (4 years) hold one of the following: regular positions or job contract* in academia/research institution/industry; or employment contract* with their parent organisation while being enrolled for Ph.D. in universities/research organisations Note:- *Applicants must hold a valid employment tenure or residual fellowship for at least 18 months starting from the application deadline. Benefits Selected candidates will receive:- A fellowship of €2,300 per month, on a pro rata basis (30 days) A travel allowance of €1,500
documents: A valid passport/visa All academic transcripts No objection certificate Joint proposal for fellowship support Proof of Current Employment/Fellowship with an active contract duration Other necessary documents

title: Government of Romania Scholarship 2026-27
amount: Full tuition fee exemption and other benefits
eligibility: International students from non-EU countries
description: The Government of Romania Scholarship 2026-27 is offered by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania. The scholarship is a prestigious financial award designed to support outstanding international students from non-EU countries pursuing undergraduate, master's, or doctoral programs. Selected candidates will receive full tuition fee exemption, free accommodation in student dormitories, and a monthly stipend depending on the study level, for the complete duration of their program.
eligibility_full: International students from non-EU countries Region Romania Award Full tuition fee exemption and other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships government-of-romania-scholarship-2026 About The Program The Government of Romania Scholarship 2026-27 is offered by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania. The scholarship is a prestigious financial award designed to support outstanding international students from non-EU countries pursuing undergraduate, master's, or doctoral programs. Selected candidates will receive full tuition fee exemption, free accommodation in student dormitories, and a monthly stipend depending on the study level, for the complete duration of their program. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an international student from a non-EU country pursue a full-time undergraduate/master's/doctoral degree program in any field have good academic results with a minimum average of 7 (according to the Romanian grading system) or equivalent "Good" score not have previously received a Romanian Government scholarship for the same study cycle Note:- Bachelor's and Master's programs are taught in the Romanian language only; a 1-year preparatory language course is provided for non-Romanian speakers. PhD programs may be offered in Romanian or English/foreign language, depending on the doctoral school. Applicants who are Romanian citizens, members of diplomatic corps, or staff of international organisations in Romania are not eligible. Benefits The selected students will receive: Exemption from registration, application processing, Romanian language testing, doctoral admission contest, and specific aptitude test fees Coverage of tuition fees for the Romanian language preparatory year full duration of the study program, within one university cycle Monthly scholarship during Bachelor’s, Master’s, or doctoral studies, within one university cycle Coverage of student dormitory accommodation costs within the allocated Ministry budget, subject to availability and coordination with the host university
documents: Certified copies of diplomas (Baccalaureate/Bachelor's/Master's/PhD if applicable) with authorised translation into English, French, Spanish, or Romanian Academic transcripts Copy of birth certificate Copy of the first three pages of the passport Curriculum vitae (English or Romanian) For PhD applicants: Letter of intent, list of academic works/publications, research project description, and agreement from doctoral tutor For applicants under 18: Parental consent declaration Medical certificate Note: All study documents must be apostilled under the Hague Convention or authenticated by the relevant authorities in the home country (e.g., ministries of education). The documents must be scanned individually, after the original, in pdf format. For more details, applicants can visit the official site.
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. The scholarship is a prestigious financial award designed to support outstanding international students from non-EU countries pursuing undergraduate, master's, or doctoral programs. Selected candidates will receive full tuition fee exemption, free accommodation in student dormitories, and a monthly stipend depending on the study level, for the complete duration of their program. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must:- be an international student from a non-EU country pursue a full-time undergraduate/master's/doctoral degree program in any field have good academic results with a minimum average of 7 (according to the Romanian grading system) or equivalent "Good" score not have previously received a Romanian Government scholarship for the same study cycle Note:- Bachelor's and Master's programs are taught in the Romanian language only; a 1-year preparatory language course is provided for non-Romanian speakers. PhD programs may be offered in Romanian or English/foreign language, depending on the doctoral school. Applicants who are Romanian citizens, members of diplomatic corps, or staff of international organisations in Romania are not eligible. Benefits The selected students will receive: Exemption from registration, application processing, Romanian language testing, doctoral admission contest, and specific aptitude test fees Coverage of tuition fees for the Romanian language preparatory year full duration of the study program, within one university cycle Monthly scholarship during Bachelor’s, Master’s, or doctoral studies, within one university cycle Coverage of student dormitory accommodation costs within the allocated Ministry budget, subject to availability and coordination with the host university Documents Certified copies of diplomas (Baccalaureate/Bachelor's/Master's/PhD if applicable) with authorised translation into English, French, Spanish, or Romanian Academic transcripts Copy of birth certificate Copy of the first three pages of the passport Curriculum vitae (English or Romanian) For PhD applicants: Letter of intent, list of academic works/publications, research project description, and agreement from doctoral tutor For applicants under 18: Parental consent declaration Medical certificate Note: All study documents must be apostilled under the Hague Convention or authenticated by the relevant authorities in the home country (e.g., ministries of education). The documents must be scanned individually, after the original, in pdf format. For more details, applicants can visit the official site. How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Click on the ‘APPLY NOW’ button. Step 4: Navigate to the 'CREATE YOUR ACCOUNT'. Step 5: Fill in the required fields and click on the ‘CREATE MY ACCOUNT’. Step 6: Verify your email address by entering the verification code sent to your registered email ID. Step 7: Once verified login with your credentials and proceed with your application. Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31st March 2026 Selection Criteria Candidates will be selected based on academic merit, educational background, and the quality of their application documents. An expert panel will review all applications and notify successful candidates. Terms and Conditions Scholarship does not cover international transport costs or domestic transport from the Romanian border to the university. Students must comply with Romanian laws and university regulations. Students must obtain a long-stay visa for Romania after receiving a Letter of Acceptance. Contact Details Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania Aleea Alexandru nr. 31, Sector 1, 011822 Bucharest Email ID: opinia_ta@mae.ro Contact Number: +40 21 319 21 08; +40 21 319 21 25 Source: https://scholarships.studyinromania.gov.ro/

title: India Science and Research Fellowship (ISRF) 2025-26
amount: Fellowship of ₹50,000 per month and other benefits
eligibility: Researchers and scientists from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand
description: The India Science and Research Fellowship (ISRF) 2025-26 is offered by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India. The fellowship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding researchers and scientists from neighbouring countries to undertake cutting-edge research and development at premier research laboratories and academic institutions in India. Selected candidates will receive a monthly fellowship of ₹50,000 and other
eligibility_full: Researchers and scientists from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand Region India Award Fellowship of ₹50,000 per month and other
benefits: Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships india-science-and-research-fellowship-isrf-2025 About The Program The India Science and Research Fellowship (ISRF) 2025-26 is offered by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India. The fellowship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding researchers and scientists from neighbouring countries to undertake cutting-edge research and development at premier research laboratories and academic institutions in India. Selected candidates will receive a monthly fellowship of ₹50,000 and other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must: be a citizen or permanent resident of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, the Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, or Thailand be actively engaged in research at a university or research institution in their country in various areas of science and technology, including engineering and medical sciences hold minimum qualifications of a PhD/M.Tech/M.Sc/MBBS with 3-5 years research or teaching experience (Applicants registered for a PhD degree in their home country may also apply) be below 45 years of age as on 31 March 2026 have a valid passport does not hold any visa for temporary or permanent residency in India not have availed the fellowship within the last 5 years Benefits The selected fellows will receive: Fellowship Allowance: ₹50,000 per month (includes accommodation and medical facilities as available to host institutes) Contingency Grant to Fellow: ₹10,000 as a one-time contingency grant Contingency Research Grant for Host Scientist: ₹20,000 as a one-time grant (through institution) Airfare: Round-trip airfare by economy class from the place of employment in their own country to the Indian host institute, through the shortest route. Local transport by road/rail between the nearest airport and the host institute in India Visa Fee: Will be reimbursed
documents: A valid passport Academic transcripts and certificates (Ph.D./M.Tech/M.Sc/MBBS) Proof of 3-5 years of research or teaching experience Curriculum vitae Research proposal
application_process: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Click on the ‘ISRF Application Form’ and download it. Step 4: Complete the application form with all required information and supporting documents. Step 5: Submit the application to the respective implementing agency in your partner country (if available) or directly to INSA, New Delhi. Step 6: Applications can be submitted as a hard copy or scan copy: Hard Copy to: The Executive Director, Indian National Science Academy (INSA), Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi-110002 Soft Copy to: Email ID: isrf.dst@insa.nic.in Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31st March 2026
selection_criteria: Selection will be based on the eligibility criteria. Final selection and fellowship decision will be made by DST, Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India, through INSA, New Delhi. Important documents Guidelines
contact: Apply Now Eligibility Researchers and scientists from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand Region India Award Fellowship of ₹50,000 per month and other benefits Deadline 31-Mar-2026 Home Scholarships india-science-and-research-fellowship-isrf-2025 About The Program The India Science and Research Fellowship (ISRF) 2025-26 is offered by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India. The fellowship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding researchers and scientists from neighbouring countries to undertake cutting-edge research and development at premier research laboratories and academic institutions in India. Selected candidates will receive a monthly fellowship of ₹50,000 and other benefits. Eligibility Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026 To be eligible, an applicant must: be a citizen or permanent resident of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, the Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, or Thailand be actively engaged in research at a university or research institution in their country in various areas of science and technology, including engineering and medical sciences hold minimum qualifications of a PhD/M.Tech/M.Sc/MBBS with 3-5 years research or teaching experience (Applicants registered for a PhD degree in their home country may also apply) be below 45 years of age as on 31 March 2026 have a valid passport does not hold any visa for temporary or permanent residency in India not have availed the fellowship within the last 5 years Benefits The selected fellows will receive: Fellowship Allowance: ₹50,000 per month (includes accommodation and medical facilities as available to host institutes) Contingency Grant to Fellow: ₹10,000 as a one-time contingency grant Contingency Research Grant for Host Scientist: ₹20,000 as a one-time grant (through institution) Airfare: Round-trip airfare by economy class from the place of employment in their own country to the Indian host institute, through the shortest route. Local transport by road/rail between the nearest airport and the host institute in India Visa Fee: Will be reimbursed Documents A valid passport Academic transcripts and certificates (Ph.D./M.Tech/M.Sc/MBBS) Proof of 3-5 years of research or teaching experience Curriculum vitae Research proposal How can you apply? Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Click on the ‘ISRF Application Form’ and download it. Step 4: Complete the application form with all required information and supporting documents. Step 5: Submit the application to the respective implementing agency in your partner country (if available) or directly to INSA, New Delhi. Step 6: Applications can be submitted as a hard copy or scan copy: Hard Copy to: The Executive Director, Indian National Science Academy (INSA), Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi-110002 Soft Copy to: Email ID: isrf.dst@insa.nic.in Apply Now Important Dates Application Deadline: 31st March 2026 Selection Criteria Selection will be based on the eligibility criteria. Final selection and fellowship decision will be made by DST, Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India, through INSA, New Delhi. Important documents Guidelines Terms and Conditions Fellows are expected to: Stay in India continuously during the accepted tenure and devote maximum working time to research work at the host institution Not engage in any other work, either paid or unpaid, during the tenure of the fellowship Follow the rules established by the host institution and DST, including prohibiting acts of research misconduct Submit a detailed research report after completion of tenure, duly endorsed by the host researcher to DST and INSA Have their own medical insurance for the period of stay in India Contact Details Department of Science & Technology, Technology Bhavan, New Mehrauli Road,, New Delhi-110 016. Contact Number: +91 11 26590215 DST Contact: Dr Charu Agarwal, Scientist D, International Cooperation Division,n Department of Science & Technology (DST), Technology Bhawan, New Mehrauli Road,d New Delhi - 110016 Email ID: c.agarwal@gov.in INSA Contact: Dr Brajesh Pandey, The Executive Director, Indian National Science Academy (INSA), Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi-110002 Email ID: isrf.dst@insa.nic.in Source: https://insaindia.res.in/
